title: Throughput drop on StreamOps pipeline
summary: Throughput dropped to 12 events/sec from a baseline of 1,200 events/sec, a 99% reduction. Consumer lag is growing rapidly at 45,000+ messages. Likely caused by upstream partition failure or producer connectivity issues.
root_cause: Upstream Kafka producer is emitting at drastically reduced rate. No errors on the Flink consumer side; the pipeline is healthy but starved for input.
timeline: T+0s: Throughput drop scenario injected by simulator
T+5s: MetricGenerator begins emitting throughput_events_per_sec values near 0-50
T+15s: Consumer lag begins accumulating as new events trickle in
T+20s: Agent detects throughput anomaly and growing consumer lag
T+25s: Diagnostic Agent confirms Flink job is RUNNING but idle
T+28s: Report Agent classifies as HIGH severity due to SLA risk

recommended_actions.action: Check upstream Kafka producer health and connectivity
recommended_actions.rationale: Flink consumer is healthy; the bottleneck is on the producer side

recommended_actions.action: Verify Kafka partition leadership and ISR status
recommended_actions.rationale: A partition leader election or ISR shrink could explain the throughput collapse

monitoring_notes: Watch throughput_events_per_sec and consumer lag. Once the producer recovers, the pipeline should clear the backlog automatically. Alert if lag does not decrease within 10 minutes of throughput recovery.
